channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Read the base-branch column.
item.description: I had three pull requests open against the same project. Sixteen, eighteen, twenty days. No review comments. CI hadn't fired on any of them. I started typing the standard seven-day-nudge message...

item.title: AI Companies Are Paying Millions for Your Old Reddit Posts. Here's Why That Should Concern You.
item.description: I am so tired of opening my code editor and seeing the same AI-generated dashboard. Same layout. Same gradient. Same components. It all looks like it came out of the same broken photocopier. Here...

item.title: Why Most AI Content Pipelines Fail at Scale (And How to Fix It)
item.description: If you've built an AI content pipeline, you've hit the wall. Not the "model isn't good enough" wall — the operational one. The pattern is always the same: You start with a script that generates a...

item.title: Terraform in Real Life — Building Your First Cloud Infrastructure Step by Step
item.description: ### Introduction If Infrastructure as Code (IaC) is the concept of managing infrastructure using code, then Terraform is one of the most powerful tools that brings it to life in the real world....

item.title: 5 Principles of Survival for Software Engineers
item.description: 5 Principles of Survival for Software Engineers Adapted from Leon Business School's "5 Principles of Survival" Your stack won’t save you. Your principles will. In the wild, survival isn’t about...

item.title: I Opened My Old Repository Again After 315 Days
item.description: Table of Contents What I Built The Project Slowly Fell Apart Revisiting the Repository Felt Weirdly Emotional Before vs After The Comeback Story My Experience with GitHub Copilot The Unexpected...

item.title: An OpenAI-Compatible Gateway for Codex Is Mostly About Cost Control
item.description: An OpenAI-compatible gateway is not exciting because it is compatible. It is exciting because compatibility lets you change the economic layer without changing the tool your team already likes....

item.title: From ThreadPoolExecutor to httpx AsyncClient: True Async Refactoring
item.description: Published on: 2026-06-06 Reading time: 6 min Tags: #python #async #performance #optimization The Problem: Fake Async The supabase-async library claimed to be async but actually wrapped synchronous...

item.title: Security Audit of 6 Python Projects: 25 Issues Found & Fixed
item.description: Published on: 2026-06-06 Reading time: 8 min Tags: #security #python #audit #devops Overview Over 3 months, I developed and audited 6 Python projects (3 bots + 3 libraries): a FastAPI + Telegram...

item.title: Kubernetes User Namespaces in 1.36 with hostUsers: false
item.description: By the end of this you will have a worker node provisioned for user namespaces and a Pod whose in-container root (UID 0) maps to a powerless UID on the host, verified by reading /proc/self/uid_map...

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description: A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited...

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description: Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video,...

item.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Microsoft's GitHub repositories have become the latest to fall victim to the ongoing Miasma self-replicating supply chain attack campaign. The incident impacted 73 Microsoft repositories across...

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager CVE-2026-20245 Flaw Actively Exploited – No Patch Available
item.description: Cisco has warned that a high-severity security flaw impacting Catalyst SD-WAN Manager has come under active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245, carries a CVSS score of 7.8...

item.title: More than a decade later, the team behind N++ is back with a multiplayer sequel
item.description: Back in 2015, the two-person studio Metanet released N++, a brutally hard 2D platformer that was a decade in the making, building off of previous releases dating back to the freeware Flash title...

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI is warping the video game release calendar
item.description: Who's afraid of the next GTA? Based on the last few days of Summer Game Fest, just about everyone. Grand Theft Auto VI hasn't been present at any of the keynote events, but its presence was felt...

item.title: Final Fantasy VII’s remake trilogy will conclude with Revelation
item.description: Square Enix has officially announced the third and final game in its Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy: Final Fantasy VII Revelation. It will release on multiple platforms simultaneously - PC, PS5,...

item.title: Thousand Token Wood: shipping a multi-agent economy on a 3B model
Control Resonant is a sequel — and also a starting point
item.description: Chronologically, Control Resonant is a sequel to 2019's Control. But in most other ways, the games aren't directly connected. To developer Remedy, they're more like two sides of the same coin....

item.title: Congress still can’t decide what to do about warrantless surveillance
item.description: The deadline to reauthorize Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is coming up a week from now on June 12th, and legislators seem no closer to reaching a deal. If this sounds...

item.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
item.description: Seller of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X doesn't consider the behavior a vulnerability.

item.title: Summer Game Fest Live 2026: The biggest news, trailers, and announcements
item.description: Geoff Keighley’s annual June showcase for videos games has officially happened, and it was a big one. Across a two hour event — and another hour for Day of the Devs — we got news about the third...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
item.description: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this...

item.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
item.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

item.title: Gone in 60 minutes
item.description: It should have been the final straw. The new power couple of editorial failure - Bari Weiss and Nick Bilton - had fired legendary 60 Minutes journalist Scott Pelley. Why? Because he dared to...

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based...

item.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
item.description: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction. Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on...

item.title: The Sonos Era 100 speaker is down to its lowest price in months
item.description: Whether you’re considering starting a Sonos speaker setup, or adding to an existing group, the Sonos Era 100 is worth picking up. The compact, capable smart speaker is currently marked down to...

item.title: This is your laptop… on AI
item.description: We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything....
